evidence: graphify/__main__.py ~4020-4027 defines NEO4J_PASSWORD/FALKORDB_PASSWORD env-var fallback specifically so --password never appears on argv/shell history (comment cites fix F-031), but exports.md's only example passes --password PASSWORD inline on the CLI and never mentions the env var
description: Neo4j/FalkorDB export example teaches passing --password inline instead of the safer env-var form the CLI itself recommends
suggested_fix: Mention NEO4J_PASSWORD / FALKORDB_PASSWORD env vars as the preferred way to supply credentials, keeping --password only as a fallback

description: README documents `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` as a manual install step for the official uv installer
fp_reason: Human-run install instructions for the official astral.sh/uv installer, not executed by any graphify automation or agent; astral.sh is the trusted upstream source for this widely-used Python tool
rule_gap: curl-pipe-sh detector should distinguish human-facing README install docs referencing well-known official installer domains from scripts/hooks actually executed by the plugin's own automation

evidence: Line reads 'or named several local subfolders to merge into one graph' with no defined count or threshold for 'several'
description: Unbounded quantifier 'several' used without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'several' with 'two or more' or otherwise state the minimum count

description: 'Run the appropriate traversal from each starting node' does not restate the BFS/DFS selection criteria defined earlier in the same file
suggested_fix: Say 'Run the BFS or DFS traversal (per the mode selected in Step 0)' instead of 'appropriate'

description: 'INFERRED: reasonable inference (shared data structure, implied dependency)' matches the R01 wordlist literally
fp_reason: 'reasonable inference' is the fixed category label for one point on a fully-enumerated 5-value discrete confidence rubric, each with its own concrete parenthetical definition -- not an unbounded instruction
rule_gap: R01's literal wordlist match should exempt terms used as fixed labels on an already-enumerated discrete scale

description: '0.75  reasonable inference (shared problem domain + similar shape, requires interpretation)' matches the R01 wordlist literally
fp_reason: Same discrete-rubric-label exemption as the line-14 occurrence in this file
rule_gap: R01's literal wordlist match should exempt terms used as fixed labels on an already-enumerated discrete scale

description: 'tell the user the corpus has no relevant vocabulary for this question' matches the R01 wordlist literally
fp_reason: 'relevant' here is verbatim text the agent is instructed to print to the user, not an unbounded instruction to the model itself
rule_gap: R01 should not flag quantifier words that appear inside a literal user-facing message string

evidence: graphify/serve.py list_tools() unconditionally registers 10 tools (query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path, list_prs, get_pr_impact, triage_prs); exports.md's Step 7d only names the first 7
description: MCP server tool list in the doc omits 3 of 10 tools the server actually exposes (list_prs, get_pr_impact, triage_prs)
suggested_fix: Add list_prs, get_pr_impact, and triage_prs to the documented MCP tool list

description: skill-agents.md's one-line summary of extraction-spec.md drops the word 'frontmatter' that the root skill.md's equivalent summary line includes, even though extraction-spec.md itself (identical across platforms) documents the frontmatter-copy rule
suggested_fix: Add 'frontmatter' back into skill-agents.md's Step-3 summary line to match skill.md and the actual reference-file content
